title: Election money flows
question: Around U.S. elections, does money actually leave the market or the country — and what do Trump wins, midterms, and wars each do to flows, returns, and volatility?
finding: The folklore is half right. The hedging cycle is real: median VIX rises into the vote and collapses to ~15 within a week of any result, cash builds pre-vote — but money then re-risks, with U.S. equity funds taking a median +6.5% of assets in the 13 weeks after the vote (6 of 6 since 2004). 'Capital flees America' is not in the flows: after both Trump wins the exit is from China funds (-14.3% of assets in 13 weeks in 2016, -20.0% in 2024, -36.3% by 26 weeks) while U.S. funds take inflows; Taiwan/Korea lag the U.S. by 12-16 points at 60 days (2024); and both times leadership flipped to Europe by day 120 (+8.2 pts median relative). Midterms beat presidential years as a signal: S&P 500 +7.5% median over the next 120 trading days, 8 of 8 since 1994, with the midterm year troughing ~-4.5% near September. Wars fit the war puzzle in 11 of 17 events (telegraphed: sag into outbreak, +3.3% median the month after; surprises bite and fade; war-spiked oil round-trips within a month). Administration volatility is episodic, not ambient: Trump I ran 21% annualized vs Biden 16%, clustered on tariff headlines (May 2019 tweet -4.2% in 5 sessions; Liberation Day -3.8%, then the largest one-day gain since 2008 on the pause). Phase 2 extends returns to 25 elections and 18 presidents since 1926: best risk-adjusted market Eisenhower (Sharpe 1.29), both Trump terms high-return/high-vol (0.83, 0.67); the midterm effect strengthens to 22 of 24 since 1930; party-flip elections crush realized vol (10 of 12) while holds do not. Gold: +29% annualized after the 2024 election vs +16% in the 2022-24 central-bank era and +11% under all of Trump I, with the gold-equity correlation turning positive. Sectors: defense is the century's most R-sensitive industry (+7.6pt R-minus-D spread; semis the most D-tilted at -7.2) and Trump-win flows rotate to materials/defense and out of staples/healthcare/utilities.
